mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
The Google Cloud SDK provides the gcloud command-line tool for managing GKE clusters.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

gcloud init

```

> This command installs the Google Cloud SDK.
> Reload the shell to access gcloud commands.
> Initialize the SDK and set up your Google Cloud project.

steps.title: Create a GKE cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the new GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates kubeconfig to use the created cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-installation setup
steps.description:
Consider enabling autoscaling, monitoring, and setting up RBAC for security.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --region=us-central1

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-cloud-logging --enable-cloud-monitoring --region=us-central1

```

> Enables cluster autoscaling.
> Enables logging and monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Use --quiet to skip confirmation prompts.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your workloads, cordon the nodes, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json > backup.json

kubectl cordon <node-name>

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all running pods.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to cordon.
> Upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that the nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:443: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates network issues or that the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status with 'gcloud container clusters list' and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this action'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user account may not have the necessary IAM permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the user has the 'Kubernetes Engine Admin' role in the Google Cloud Console.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Deploy an application using 'kubectl apply -f <your-app.yaml>' to create resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified cluster does not exist or the context is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster name and context with 'gcloud container clusters list' and 'kubectl config get-contexts'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new GKE Autopilot features, improved workload identity, and enhanced security policies.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy cluster autoscaler, which will be removed in future versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, review the new security policies and adjust configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and configured.
